title: iberis amara subsp. decipiens
intro: Iberis amara subsp. decipiens, also known as Rock Cress, is a flowering plant species within the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its clusters of small, white, or pinkish flowers and its ability to thrive in rocky, dry areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Rock Cress include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and ease digestive issues due to its antimicrobial and antispasmodic effects. Rock Cress can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used in salads or as a garnish, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
